summary:
The ECG indicates right atrial enlargement through tall P-waves without signs of bundle branch block or ventricular abnormalities; this pattern supports diagnosing as \(P\) pulmonale due to likely underlying right-sided cardiac issues. Therefore,

To summarize Agent1’s reasoning into two concise sentences:

Tall P-waves in Lead II suggest right atrial enlargement, ruling out other causes like bundle branch blocks; thus, the patient most probably has P pulmonale associated with potential right heart failure. Hence,
\[ C \] should be selected for confirmation. 

This summary captures both critical aspects—tall P-wave significance and exclusionary findings—that lead to identifying P pulmonale accurately from provided options.
